fingerprints.method_out_of_state: IdentoGO Out-of-State Live Scan (adds ~$39.95 fee) OR FD-258 hard card via mail (tip: moisturize hands for days prior to reduce smudge rejects)

seo.title: Maine Audiologist License Reciprocity - How to Transfer Your License (2026)
seo.description: Already licensed as a audiologist? Here's whether your license transfers to Maine - compact status, endorsement steps, fees, and processing time.
seo.keywords: audiologist license reciprocity maine, how to transfer audiologist license to maine, audiologist compact maine, maine audiologist endorsement application

board.name: Board of Speech-Language Pathology and Audiology
board.parent_dept: Maine Department of Professional and Financial Regulation
board.mailing_address:
Department of Professional and Financial Regulation
Office of Professional and Occupational Regulation
35 State House Station
Augusta, ME 04333-0035

license_types.title: AuD, CCC-A
license_types.description: The standard credential to practice as a audiologist in Maine.

steps.title: Create a User Account
steps.description: Navigate to the Maine Online Licensing System (https://www.pfr.maine.gov/ALMSOnline/ALMSQuery/Welcome.aspx?board=SLPA) and click 'Click Here to Register' to establish your personal user account.

steps.title: Initiate New Application
steps.description: Log in to your newly created account on the Maine Online Licensing System. Select 'Apply for a New License' from the menu and choose 'Audiologist' as your profession.

steps.title: Complete Online Application
steps.description: Fill out all mandatory sections of the application form within the Maine Online Licensing System, providing comprehensive details on your personal information, education, and professional experience, including any required supervised practice.

steps.title: Upload Supporting Documentation
steps.description: Electronically submit all necessary documents as specified in the application checklist, which typically includes official transcripts from your graduate program and other verification forms, directly through the Maine Online Licensing System.

steps.title: Pay Application Fee
steps.description: Remit the non-refundable $250 application fee using a credit or debit card through the secure payment portal within the Maine Online Licensing System.

steps.title: Submit Application
steps.description: Carefully review all entered information and uploaded documents for accuracy and completeness before formally submitting your application online to the Maine Board of Speech-Language Pathology and Audiology.

documents.detail: Sent directly from your educational institution.
Official verification of passing the Praxis Exam in Audiology.

provisional_text: Many states offer a limited permit or provisional license for new graduates waiting to take the Praxis. Check specific board rules for supervisory requirements.
reciprocity_text: Audiologists licensed in another state can often apply via endorsement by providing license verification and current certification.

faqs.question: How much does a Maine audiologist license cost?
faqs.answer: The initial application and licensing fee varies, often around $100 to $200. Check the specific fee breakdown above.

faqs.question: Is national certification required?
faqs.answer: Yes, passing the Praxis Exam in Audiology is a standard requirement for initial licensure across almost all states.

faqs.question: Does Maine have reciprocity for audiologists?
faqs.answer: Yes. Maine participates in the ASLP-IC. If your home state and license qualify for compact privilege, you can typically practice in Maine without a separate endorsement application.

faqs.question: How long does it take to transfer my audiologist license to Maine?
faqs.answer: Most endorsement applications take 4 to 6 Weeks. If you qualify for an active compact privilege, there may be no separate wait to start practicing.

faqs.question: Can I start working in Maine while my endorsement application is processing?
faqs.answer: A temporary or provisional credential is available while the full endorsement application is pending.

reciprocity.notes: Audiologists licensed in another state can often apply via endorsement by providing license verification and current certification.

hero.title: Become a Audiologist in Maine
hero.dek: Applicants for Audiologist licensure in Maine must apply through the Maine Online Licensing System, managed by the Office of Professional and Occupational Regulation (OPOR).

official_links.registration_steps: Visit the Maine Online Licensing System, click 'Click Here to Register' for new users, and follow the prompts to create your user account.
